response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills and experience with event-driven systems, but lacks direct exposure to multi-agent AI architectures, fintech infrastructure, or regulated environments. Their recent role at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure, but it is unclear if this includes production-scale agentic systems. The resume also does not highlight fintech-specific experience, real-time credit risk, or compliance frameworks, which are critical for this role.
strengths: Strong backend and distributed systems expertise with event-driven architectures (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby media)
Experience with AI-agent frameworks and developer tooling (e.g., MCP, Model Context Protocol at re:cinq)
Proven track record of scaling engineering practices (e.g., TDD, CI/CD at RIDE Capital, Altom Consulting)
concerns: No demonstrated production experience with multi-agent AI systems in fintech or regulated environments
Limited fintech-specific background (e.g., credit risk, payment flows, embedded finance)
No clear evidence of leading engineering teams to 30+ people or designing governance frameworks for outsourced engineering
